D1 Optics L4 Analyze Reference

Calcite Birefringence

send a ray into calcite and watch it split into ordinary and extraordinary rays

Anisotropic crystals like calcite have two refractive indices: n_o for light polarised perpendicular to the optical axis, n_e for light along it. The two rays follow different paths.

Slide the incidence angle. The slate o-ray and crimson e-ray separate inside the crystal, exit parallel, and emerge as two distinct beams below.
